text.heading: Enugu raises panel on free maternal health, childcare
text.p: The Enugu State Government has inaugurated the State Committee on Free Maternal and Child Care with Dr. Martin Chukwuike, the Commissioner for Health as Chairman.

In an inauguration speech at the Government House, Enugu State Governor, Sullivan Chime, who was represented by his Deputy, Sunday Onyebuchi, noted that the Free Maternal and Child Health Care programme in the State is a road map for accelerating maternal and child mortality reduction in line with the Millennium Development Goals.
He added that the major causes of mortality in Nigerian children included malaria, preventable diseases, particularly measles, diarrhea and mal-nutrition.
The Governor said that these deaths are closely related to poverty and ignorance.

"The poor families being unable to afford the very little but much needed money to go to hospital for children’s clinics, antenatal clinics, delivery and post-natal clinics, are left with no choice except what they can afford."
He regretted that what they could afford most of the time would be going to traditional birth attendants and drug shops manned by untrained people.
The Governor added that it was the challenge posed by this problem that the State government has decided to address.

He enumerated some objectives and targets of the programme which included acceleration and reduction of maternal and child mortality to attain the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs) to reduce neonatal mortality rate by five per cent per annum and to reduce the incidence of malaria, preventable diseases, acute respiratory infections, among others.

In his speech, the Chairman of the Committee, Dr. Martin Chukwunewike described the free medical programme as the first of its kind and a demonstration of Governor Chime’s special interest in the welfare of women and children in the State.
Chukwuweike, who is also the State Commissioner for Health, on behalf of other members thanked the Governor for finding them fit for the job.
"We believe that once the programme is flagged off it will reach all the State owned health institutions,"said Chukwuweike.
He pledged their total commitment to the success of the programme, stressing that they would work hard to ensure that the programme succeeded.
"We will make sure that all the policies, all the facilities needed for it to be well implemented would be put in place.
We promise that we will not fail you with the support of the Committee members and the cooperation of Government."
